mechanism_narrative: Persistently near-zero federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS below 0.25%) signals an accommodative monetary stance that lowers the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of other conditions.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.12% as of 2015-06, near zero-lower-bound
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 20-day forward SPX return falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window, or mean forward return during sub-0.25% FEDFUNDS periods is not significantly greater than unconditional mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in UNRATE crossing below a 3-month moving-average threshold signals labor market tightening that supports consumer income and corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter (60 trading days).
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by more than 0.2pp over trailing 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE reported at elevated level with historical downtrend since 2010 recovery
failure_condition: In triggered episodes, 60-day forward SPX returns are negative or not statistically different from zero (t-test p > 0.05) across at least two independent historical triggering windows.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in RSAFS (retail sales) and HOUST (housing starts) reflects broad-based consumer and residential demand strength that is not captured by either series alone; simultaneous positive month-over-month growth in both signals a reinforcing demand impulse that lifts S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS MoM growth > 0.3% AND HOUST MoM growth > 2% simultaneously
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with strengthening consumer and housing demand
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes produce forward 20-day SPX returns statistically indistinguishable from episodes where only one series is positive (difference-in-means p > 0.10), indicating no genuine interaction effect.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime where INDPRO growth decelerates but remains positive, industrial production momentum loses predictive power for equities because supply-side capacity constraints dominate, causing the historically positive INDPRO-to-SPX relationship to weaken or invert over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO YoY growth decelerates for 3 consecutive months while remaining above 0%
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 100.3 index level with moderate growth trajectory in mid-2015
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with decelerating industrial momentum
failure_condition: During decelerating-INDPRO episodes, 60-day forward SPX returns remain positive and significantly above zero (p < 0.05), contradicting the predicted negative drift.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) is a continuously informative always-on signal of near-term economic momentum, with each positive print associated with modest positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days due to reinforcement of growth expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 130910 thousand persons, trending upward through 2015
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS monthly change and 5-day forward SPX return is below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or sign hit-rate falls below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: A crossing of CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation above a 2% threshold signals rising inflationary pressure that raises expectations of monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 217.541 with subdued YoY inflation around 0.1-0.3% in mid-2015
regime_hint: rising inflation regime preceding monetary tightening
failure_condition: Following threshold crossings, 20-day forward SPX returns are not significantly negative (p > 0.05) across at least two historical crossing episodes, or hit-rate for negative returns is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in GDPC1 quarterly growth alongside falling RSAFS and HOUST signals a broad-based demand contraction across consumption, investment, and housing channels that is more predictive of a sustained equity drawdown over 60 trading days than any single series in isolation.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth below 1.5% AND RSAFS MoM growth negative AND HOUST MoM decline > 5%
regime_hint: broad demand-side slowdown preceding recessionary risk
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes show 60-day forward SPX returns statistically indistinguishable from unconditional baseline (p > 0.10), indicating the multi-series interaction adds no incremental predictive power over single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-interest-rate regime (FEDFUNDS near 0%), positive INDPRO surprises are amplified in their equity market impact because monetary policy cannot offset supply-side weakness with further easing, making industrial production momentum a stronger short-horizon (5-day) driver of S&P 500 returns than in normal-rate regimes.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS below 0.25% AND INDPRO MoM growth positive
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.12% with limited policy easing headroom in mid-2015
failure_condition: The interaction coefficient between FEDFUNDS regime and INDPRO surprise is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in a regression on 5-day forward SPX returns, or effect size in ZLB regime is smaller than in normal-rate regime.
